preamble: Voicing of the voiceless postalveolar affricate [tʃ] to [dʒ] often occurs in intervocalic positions or as a result of dialectal variation.

examples.note: In some dialects, /tʃ/ can voice or merge with voiced counterparts.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
